content.text:
# Extracted Testable Requirements by Spec File

---

## Spec 01: Types and Schema Validation (`types.ts`, `schema-validator.ts`)

### types.ts — Interfaces (no runtime logic)

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| T1 | `WorkflowSpec.name`: string |
| T2 | `WorkflowSpec.description`: string |
| T3 | `WorkflowSpec.version`: optional string |
| T4 | `WorkflowSpec.input`: optional `Record<string, unknown>` (JSON Schema object) |
| T5 | `WorkflowSpec.output`: optional `Record<string, unknown>` (JSON Schema object) |
| T6 | `WorkflowSpec.triggerTurn`: optional boolean, default `true` |
| T7 | `WorkflowSpec.steps`: `Record<string, StepSpec>` — insertion order = declaration order = execution order |
| T8 | `WorkflowSpec.source`: `"user" | "project"` — set by discovery, not YAML |
| T9 | `WorkflowSpec.filePath`: string — set by discovery, not YAML |
| T10 | `StepSpec.agent`: string, required in phase 1 |
| T11 | `StepSpec.model`: optional string |
| T12 | `StepSpec.input`: optional `Record<string, unknown>`, values may contain `${{ }}` expressions |
| T13 | `StepOutputSpec.format`: optional `"json" | "text"`, default `"text"` |
| T14 | `StepOutputSpec.schema`: optional string, path to JSON Schema file relative to workflow spec |
| T15 | `AgentSpec.name`: string |
| T16 | `AgentSpec` optional fields: `description`, `systemPrompt`, `model`, `thinking`, `tools` (string[]), `extensions` (string[]), `skills` (string[]), `output` (string) |
| T17 | `ExecutionState.input`: unknown |
| T18 | `ExecutionState.steps`: `Record<string, StepResult>` |
| T19 | `ExecutionState.status`: `"running" | "completed" | "failed"` |
| T20 | `StepResult` fields: `step`, `agent`, `status` (`"completed" | "failed" | "skipped"`), optional `output`, optional `textOutput`, optional `sessionLog`, `usage` (StepUsage), `durationMs`, optional `error` |
| T21 | `StepUsage` fields: `input`, `output`, `cacheRead`, `cacheWrite`, `cost`, `turns` (all numbers) |
| T22 | `WorkflowResult` fields: `workflow`, `runId`, `status` (`"completed" | "failed"`), `steps`, optional `output`, `totalUsage`, `totalDurationMs`, `runDir` (absolute path) |
| T23 | `ExpressionScope` has `input: unknown` and `steps: Record<string, StepResult>` |

### schema-validator.ts — Exports & Behavior

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| V1 | **Export**: `validate(schema: Record<string, unknown>, data: unknown, label: string): unknown` |
| V2 | **Export**: `validateFromFile(schemaPath: string, data: unknown, label: string): unknown` |
| V3 | **Export**: `class ValidationError extends Error` with `label: string` and `errors: Ajv.ErrorObject[]` |
| V4 | `validate()` returns validated data on success (pass-through) |
| V5 | `validate()` throws `ValidationError` on failure |
| V6 | `ValidationError.message` format: `"Validation failed for <label>: <error1>; <error2>; ..."` |
| V7 | Each error includes JSON path and message, e.g. `"/description: must be string"` |
| V8 | AJV configured with `allErrors: true` — reports ALL errors, not just the first |
| V9 | AJV configured with `strict: false` — accepts JSON Schema draft-07 and later without warnings |
| V10 | `validate()` with empty schema `{}` accepts any data |
| V11 | `validate()` reports multiple missing required fields when `allErrors: true` |
| V12 | `validateFromFile()` reads file with `fs.readFileSync`, parses with `JSON.parse`, delegates to `validate()` |
| V13 | `validateFromFile()` throws `Error("Schema file not found: <path>")` if file doesn't exist |
| V14 | `validateFromFile()` throws `Error("Invalid JSON in schema file: <path>: <parse error>")` if JSON is invalid |
| V15 | `label` is a human-readable identifier used in error messages |

---

## Spec 02: Expression Evaluator (`expression.ts`)

### Exports

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| E1 | **Export**: `resolveExpressions(value: unknown, scope: ExpressionScope): unknown` |
| E2 | **Export**: `resolveExpression(expr: string, scope: ExpressionScope): unknown` — expr WITHOUT `${{ }}` delimiters |
| E3 | **Export**: `class ExpressionError extends Error` with `expression: string` and `reason: string` |

### Expression Syntax & Resolution

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| E4 | Pattern: `${{ <path> }}` — whitespace inside braces is trimmed |
| E5 | Regex: `/\$\{\{\s*(.*?)\s*\}\}/g` |
| E6 | `resolveExpression` splits path on `.` and walks scope object segment by segment |
| E7 | If any segment resolves to `undefined` or `null`, throw `ExpressionError` |
| E8 | **Whole-value expression**: if entire string is `${{ expr }}` (no surrounding text), return resolved value with original type (object, array, number, etc.) |
| E9 | **Embedded expression**: if `${{ expr }}` appears within other text, stringify resolved value — `JSON.stringify()` for objects/arrays, `String()` for primitives |
| E10 | **Multiple expressions** in one string: all resolved left-to-right |
| E11 | **No expression**: strings without `${{ }}` returned as-is |
| E12 | **Recursive resolution**: objects walked recursively, only string values checked for expressions |
| E13 | **Array resolution**: array elements resolved recursively |
| E14 | **Non-string primitives** (number, boolean, null): returned as-is |
| E15 | `${{ input.description }}` → `scope.input.description` |
| E16 | `${{ steps.diagnose.output }}` → whole output object |
| E17 | `${{ steps.diagnose.output.fixLocation.file }}` → nested property access |
| E18 | `${{ steps.diagnose.status }}` → string like `"completed"` |
| E19 | `${{ steps.diagnose.usage.cost }}` → number like `0.03` |

### Error Messages

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| E20 | Error includes full expression text |
| E21 | Error includes which segment failed |
| E22 | Error includes context about step status if path starts with `steps.` |
| E23 | Example: `"Expression error in '${{ steps.verify.output }}': step 'verify' has not been executed yet"` |
| E24 | Example: `"Expression error in '${{ input.nonexistent }}': property 'nonexistent' is undefined on input"` |

---

## Spec 03: Workflow Spec Parsing and Discovery (`workflow-spec.ts`, `workflow-discovery.ts`)

### workflow-spec.ts

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| S1 | **Export**: `parseWorkflowSpec(content: string, filePath: string, source: "user" | "project"): WorkflowSpec` |
| S2 | **Export**: `class WorkflowSpecError extends Error` with `filePath: string` and `reason: string` |
| S3 | Parses YAML with the `yaml` package. Throws `WorkflowSpecError` on invalid YAML |
| S4 | `name`: string, required. Error if missing or not a string |
| S5 | `steps`: object with at least one key, required. Error if missing, not an object, or empty |
| S6 | Each step: `agent` string required (phase 1). Error if missing |
| S7 | Each step: `input` if present must be an object, error if not |
| S8 | Each step: `output` if present must be an object. If `output.schema` present, must be a string |
| S9 | Each step: `model` if present must be a string |
| S10 | `description` defaults to `""` |
| S11 | `triggerTurn` defaults to `true` |
| S12 | `source` and `filePath` set from arguments, not from YAML |
| S13 | Step order preserved (YAML insertion order = iteration order) |
| S14 | Error message format: `"Invalid workflow spec (<filePath>): <reason>"` |
| S15 | Specific errors: `"'name' is required"`, `"'steps' must be a non-empty object"`, `"step '<name>' is missing 'agent'"`, `"step '<name>' input must be an object"`, `"invalid YAML: <parse error>"` |

### workflow-discovery.ts

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| D1 | **Export**: `discoverWorkflows(cwd: string): WorkflowSpec[]` |
| D2 | **Export**: `findWorkflow(name: string, cwd: string): WorkflowSpec \| undefined` |
| D3 | Scans project dir: `path.join(cwd, ".pi", "workflows")` with `source: "project"` |
| D4 | Scans user dir: `path.join(os.homedir(), ".pi", "agent", "workflows")` with `source: "user"` |
| D5 | Finds files matching `*.workflow.yaml` (non-recursive) |
| D6 | Project-level specs take precedence over user-level specs with the same `name` |
| D7 | If parsing fails, log to stderr: `"[pi-workflows] Warning: skipping <path>: <error>"` and continue (no throw) |
| D8 | Returns list sorted by name |
| D9 | Returns empty array when no workflow directories exist |
| D10 | `findWorkflow()` calls `discoverWorkflows()`, returns first spec matching name |
| D11 | `findWorkflow()` returns `undefined` if not found |

---

## Spec 04: Subprocess Dispatch (`dispatch.ts`)

### Exports & Types

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P1 | **Export**: `dispatch(step: StepSpec, agentSpec: AgentSpec, prompt: string, options: DispatchOptions): Promise<StepResult>` |
| P2 | `DispatchOptions` has: `cwd`, `sessionLogDir`, `stepName`, optional `signal` (AbortSignal), optional `onEvent` callback |
| P3 | `ProcessEvent` has: `type`, `raw`, optional `toolName`, `toolArgs`, `messageText`, `usage` |

### CLI Argument Construction

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P4 | Always includes `--mode json` |
| P5 | Always includes `--session-dir <sessionLogDir>` |
| P6 | Model: step overrides agent spec (`step.model ?? agentSpec.model`) |
| P7 | If model set and `agentSpec.thinking` exists, model arg is `${model}:${thinking}` |
| P8 | Tool filtering: items with `/`, `.ts`, or `.js` treated as extension paths → `--extension`; others as builtin → `--tools` (comma-joined) |
| P9 | Extension scoping: if `agentSpec.extensions !== undefined`, push `--no-extensions` then each `--extension` |
| P10 | Prompt passed via `-p` arg |
| P11 | Long prompts (> 8000 chars): write to temp file, use `-p @<tmpfile>` |
| P12 | System prompt: write to temp file, pass via `--append-system-prompt <file>` inserted before `-p` |

### Process Spawning & Event Handling

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P13 | Spawns `pi` with constructed args via `child_process.spawn` |
| P14 | Process `stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"]` |
| P15 | Stdout is newline-delimited JSON, processed line by line |
| P16 | Unparseable lines are skipped (no error) |
| P17 | `message_end` events with `role === "assistant"` increment `usage.turns` |
| P18 | Usage fields accumulated: `input`, `output`, `cacheRead`, `cacheWrite`, `cost` (from `usage.cost.total`) |
| P19 | Text extracted from last assistant message content (type `"text"` parts) |
| P20 | `onEvent` callback fired for each parsed event with extracted fields |

### Text Extraction

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P21 | `extractText(content)`: scans array for `{ type: "text", text: "..." }`, returns first text |
| P22 | Returns `""` if content is not an array or has no text parts |

### Tool Args Preview

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P23 | `extractToolArgsPreview(args)`: checks keys `command`, `path`, `pattern`, `query`, `task` in order |
| P24 | Returns first matching string value, truncated to 60 chars (57 + "...") |
| P25 | Returns `""` if args is not an object or no matching key |

### Cancellation

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P26 | If `signal` provided and aborted, kill proc with `SIGTERM` |
| P27 | If proc not killed after 3 seconds, send `SIGKILL` |
| P28 | If signal already aborted at start, kill immediately |
| P29 | Signal listener uses `{ once: true }` |

### Result Construction

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| P30 | `status`: `"completed"` if exit code 0, `"failed"` otherwise |
| P31 | `error`: stderr content if non-empty, else `"Process exited with code <N>"` (only on failure) |
| P32 | `textOutput`: text from last assistant message |
| P33 | `durationMs`: `Date.now() - startTime` |
| P34 | Temp files cleaned up after process exits (`fs.rmSync(tmpDir, ...)`) |
| P35 | Error detection: exit code is the definitive signal — no pattern-matching on output |

---

## Spec 05: State Persistence and TUI (`state.ts`, `tui.ts`)

### state.ts Exports

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| ST1 | **Export**: `generateRunId(workflowName: string): string` — format: `<name>-<yyyymmdd>-<hhmmss>-<4 hex>` |
| ST2 | **Export**: `initRunDir(cwd: string, runId: string): string` — creates `.pi/workflow-runs/<runId>/`, `sessions/`, `outputs/`; returns absolute path |
| ST3 | **Export**: `writeState(runDir: string, state: ExecutionState): void` — `JSON.stringify(state, null, 2)` to `state.json` |
| ST4 | **Atomic write**: write to `.state.json.tmp`, then `fs.renameSync` to `state.json` |
| ST5 | **Export**: `readState(runDir: string): ExecutionState \| null` — returns `null` if file doesn't exist |
| ST6 | **Export**: `writeStepOutput(runDir: string, stepName: string, output: unknown): void` — writes to `outputs/<stepName>.json` |
| ST7 | **Export**: `writeMetrics(runDir: string, steps: Record<string, StepResult>): void` — writes `metrics.json` |
| ST8 | **Export**: `buildResult(spec, runId, runDir, state, status): WorkflowResult` |
| ST9 | **Export**: `formatResult(result: WorkflowResult): string` |
| ST10 | **Export**: `aggregateUsage(steps: Record<string, StepResult>): StepUsage` |

### state.ts Behavior

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| ST11 | `generateRunId` example: `bugfix-20260312-214041-a3f2` |
| ST12 | `generateRunId` includes workflow name as prefix |
| ST13 | `generateRunId` generates unique IDs (random suffix) |
| ST14 | `initRunDir` creates parent `.pi/workflow-runs/` if it doesn't exist |
| ST15 | `writeState`/`readState` round-trips `ExecutionState` correctly |
| ST16 | `buildResult` aggregates usage across steps, computes total duration |
| ST17 | `buildResult` sets output to last completed step's output (or explicit workflow output) |
| ST18 | `aggregateUsage` sums `input`, `output`, `cacheRead`, `cacheWrite`, `cost`, `turns` across all steps |
| ST19 | `formatResult` for completed: `"Workflow '<name>' completed (<N> steps, <duration>, $<cost>)"` |
| ST20 | `formatResult` for failed: `"Workflow '<name>' failed at step '<step>' (<N>/<total> steps, <duration>, $<cost>)"` |
| ST21 | `formatResult` shows per-step lines: `✓` for completed, `✗` for failed |
| ST22 | `formatResult` includes session logs path |
| ST23 | Duration formatting: `<60s` → `42s`; `<60m` → `1m32s`; `≥60m` → `1h02m` |
| ST24 | Cost formatting: `$0.03`, `$1.24`, `$12.50` |

### tui.ts Exports & Behavior

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| TU1 | **Export**: `createProgressWidget(widgetState: ProgressWidgetState): (tui: TUI, theme: Theme) => Component & { dispose?(): void }` |
| TU2 | `ProgressWidgetState` has: `spec`, `state`, optional `currentStep`, `startTime` |
| TU3 | Widget renders `render(width: number): string[]` |
| TU4 | Line 1: `● <workflow name>  step <current>/<total>  <elapsed>` |
| TU5 | Completed step: `  ✓ <name>  <duration>  $<cost>` |
| TU6 | Running step: `  ▸ <name>  <elapsed>...` |
| TU7 | Pending step: `  · <name>` |
| TU8 | Failed step: `  ✗ <name>  <duration>  <error preview>` |
| TU9 | Pulsing `●` alternates between accent and dim color every 800ms |
| TU10 | Uses `setInterval`, cleared in `dispose()` |
| TU11 | All lines truncated to `width` |
| TU12 | Theme usage: `accent` for running indicator, `success` for ✓, `dim` for ·, `error` for ✗, `dim` for time/cost, `bold` for workflow name |

---

## Spec 06: Workflow Executor (`workflow-executor.ts`)

### Export

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| X1 | **Export**: `executeWorkflow(spec: WorkflowSpec, input: unknown, options: ExecuteOptions): Promise<WorkflowResult>` |
| X2 | `ExecuteOptions` has: `ctx` (ExtensionContext), `pi` (ExtensionAPI), optional `signal`, `loadAgent: (name: string) => AgentSpec` |

### Execution Sequence

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| X3 | Step 1: Validate input against `spec.input` schema if defined. Throws `ValidationError` before any steps run |
| X4 | Step 2: Generate run ID via `generateRunId`, init run dir via `initRunDir` |
| X5 | Step 3: Initialize `ExecutionState` with `status: "running"`, empty `steps`, provided `input` |
| X6 | Step 3b: If `ctx.hasUI`, set TUI widget via `ctx.ui.setWidget("workflow-progress", ...)` |
| X7 | Step 4: Execute steps in declared order (sequential, phase 1) |
| X8 | Before each step: check `signal?.aborted` — if so, mark step failed with `"Workflow cancelled"`, break |
| X9 | Before each step: update widget `currentStep` and re-set widget |
| X10 | Resolve `${{ }}` expressions in `stepSpec.input` using current scope (`input` + completed `steps`) |
| X11 | If expression resolution fails: record step as failed with error message, set `state.status = "failed"`, break |
| X12 | Load agent spec via `options.loadAgent(stepSpec.agent)` |
| X13 | Build prompt from resolved input via `buildPrompt()` |
| X14 | Dispatch subprocess via `dispatch()` with cwd, sessionLogDir, stepName, signal, onEvent |

### Output Validation

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| X15 | If step has `output.schema` and status is `"completed"`: resolve schema path relative to workflow spec file |
| X16 | Check for output file at `<runDir>/outputs/<stepName>.json` |
| X17 | If output file exists: parse JSON, validate against schema via `validateFromFile`, set `result.output` |
| X18 | If output file doesn't exist: try to parse `textOutput` as JSON, validate, set `result.output`, write via `writeStepOutput` |
| X19 | If both fail: mark step as failed with `"Step '<name>' has output schema but no valid JSON output was produced"` |
| X20 | If no schema but `result.output` exists: persist via `writeStepOutput` |

### State & Finalization

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| X21 | After each step: store result in `state.steps`, call `writeState(runDir, state)` |
| X22 | **Fail fast**: if any step fails, set `state.status = "failed"`, break loop |
| X23 | After loop: if `state.status` still `"running"`, set to `"completed"` |
| X24 | Final `writeState()` and `writeMetrics()` calls |
| X25 | If `ctx.hasUI`: clear widget via `ctx.ui.setWidget("workflow-progress", undefined)` |
| X26 | Build result via `buildResult()` |
| X27 | Call `pi.sendMessage()` with `{ customType: "workflow-result", content: formatResult(result), display: "verbose" }` |
| X28 | `triggerTurn` passed to `sendMessage` — `true` unless `spec.triggerTurn` is explicitly `false` |
| X29 | Return `WorkflowResult` |

### Prompt Construction

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| X30 | `buildPrompt()`: if resolvedInput is non-empty object, format as `## Input\n\`\`\`json\n<JSON>\n\`\`\`` |
| X31 | If resolvedInput is a string, use it directly |
| X32 | If step has `output.format === "json"` or `output.schema`: append output instructions with output file path |
| X33 | Output instruction: `"**Output:** Write your result as valid JSON to: <path>"` |
| X34 | If `output.schema` present: append `"The output must conform to the JSON Schema at: <schemaPath>"` |

### Schema Path Resolution

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| X35 | If schema path is absolute, return as-is |
| X36 | If relative, resolve against directory containing the workflow spec file |

### Error Handling Table

| # | Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|---|
| X37 | Workflow input fails validation | Throw `ValidationError` before any steps |
| X38 | Expression resolution fails | Step failed, stop execution |
| X39 | Agent not found | `loadAgent` returns minimal spec, pi runs with defaults |
| X40 | Subprocess exits non-zero | Step failed, stderr or "Process exited with code N", stop |
| X41 | Output schema validation fails | Step failed, validation error, stop |
| X42 | Output file not written | Try parse textOutput as JSON; if fails, step failed |
| X43 | AbortSignal fired | SIGTERM current step, step failed with "Workflow cancelled", stop |
| X44 | Run directory creation fails | Throw filesystem error |

---

## Spec 07: Extension Entry Point, Package Setup, and Demo (`index.ts`, `package.json`, demo files)

### package.json

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| PK1 | `name: "pi-workflows"`, `version: "0.1.0"` |
| PK2 | `"type": "module"` — ESM |
| PK3 | `pi.extensions` points to `["./index.ts"]` |
| PK4 | `"keywords": ["pi-package"]` |
| PK5 | Dependencies: `ajv ^8.17.1`, `yaml ^2.7.1` |
| PK6 | Peer deps: `@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent`, `@mariozechner/pi-tui`, `@sinclair/typebox` |
| PK7 | Test script: `node --experimental-strip-types --test *.test.ts` |
| PK8 | No build step — pi loads `.ts` directly |

### index.ts — Tool Registration

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| I1 | Extension default export is `ExtensionFactory` |
| I2 | Registers tool named `"workflow"` |
| I3 | Tool has `label: "Workflow"` |
| I4 | Tool parameters: `workflow` (string, required), `input` (unknown, optional) |
| I5 | Tool `execute`: calls `findWorkflow(params.workflow, ctx.cwd)` |
| I6 | If workflow not found: return text `"Workflow '<name>' not found. Available workflows: <list>"` |
| I7 | On success: return text with `formatToolResult(result)` and `details: result` |
| I8 | On error: return text `"Workflow '<name>' failed: <error>"` |

### index.ts — Command Registration

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| I9 | Registers command `"workflow"` |
| I10 | `getArgumentCompletions`: returns `["run", "list", "status"]` filtered by prefix |
| I11 | `/workflow list`: discovers and displays workflows with source tag and description |
| I12 | `/workflow list` with no workflows: notify `"No workflows found in .pi/workflows/ or ~/.pi/agent/workflows/"` |
| I13 | `/workflow run <name>`: finds workflow, executes it |
| I14 | `/workflow run` without name: notify `"Usage: /workflow run <name> [--input '<json>']"` |
| I15 | `/workflow run <name> --input '<json>'`: parses JSON input |
| I16 | `/workflow run` with invalid JSON input: notify `"Invalid JSON for --input"` |
| I17 | `/workflow run` with unknown workflow: notify `"Workflow '<name>' not found."` |
| I18 | `/workflow status`: notify `"No workflow currently running."` |
| I19 | Unknown subcommand: notify `"Unknown subcommand: <cmd>. Use: list, run, status"` |
| I20 | Default subcommand (no args): `list` |

### parseAgentFrontmatter

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| I21 | Reads `.md` file, extracts YAML frontmatter between `---` markers |
| I22 | Content after frontmatter is the `systemPrompt` |
| I23 | Maps frontmatter fields: `name`, `description`, `model`, `thinking`, `tools`, `extensions`, `skills`, `output` |
| I24 | If no frontmatter: returns `{ name: path.basename(filePath, ".md") }` |
| I25 | `name` defaults to `frontmatter.name || path.basename(filePath, ".md")` |
| I26 | `systemPrompt` set to trimmed content after frontmatter, or `undefined` if empty |

### Agent Loader

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| I27 | `createAgentLoader(cwd)` returns a function `(name: string) => AgentSpec` |
| I28 | Search order: `.pi/agents/<name>.md` (project), then `~/.pi/agent/agents/<name>.md` (user) |
| I29 | If found: parse frontmatter and return `AgentSpec` |
| I30 | If not found: return `{ name }` (minimal spec, pi runs with defaults) |

### Demo Workflow

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| DM1 | `demo/explore-summarize.workflow.yaml`: name `explore-summarize`, 2 steps (`explore`, `summarize`) |
| DM2 | Input schema: `path` (string, required), `question` (string, optional) |
| DM3 | Step `explore` uses agent `demo-explorer`, receives `${{ input.path }}` and `${{ input.question }}` |
| DM4 | Step `summarize` uses agent `demo-summarizer`, receives `${{ steps.explore.textOutput }}` and `${{ input.question }}` |
| DM5 | `demo/agents/demo-explorer.md`: tools `[read, bash, ls, find, grep]` |
| DM6 | `demo/agents/demo-summarizer.md`: tools `[read]` |

### Acceptance Criteria

| # | Requirement |
|---|---|
| AC1 | Demo completes successfully when given a valid path |
| AC2 | Demo fails with clear validation error when path is missing |
| AC3 | Shows progress widget during execution (interactive mode) |
| AC4 | Writes `state.json` to run directory with `status: "completed"` |
| AC5 | Writes session logs for both steps |
| AC6 | Injects result into conversation (`sendMessage` called) |
| AC7 | Returns structured output accessible to the main agent |
| AC8 | Works both as tool invocation and `/workflow run` command |
